steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The word “coincided” explicitly means the two broods arrived simultaneously. The blank must name the phenomenon of two separate things with different cycles meeting at the same moment—an event so rare it happens only once every 221 years.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank refers to the event when both cicada broods emerged simultaneously in 2024 — the sentence calls it something "that happens only once every 221 years," so the blank names the rare phenomenon of two separate cycles meeting at one moment. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — A succession: one thing following another in sequence — this implies one brood came after the other, not at the same time; "coincided" means simultaneous, not sequential. ✘
  • Choice B — A division: a splitting apart or separation — the opposite of what happened; the two broods came together, they did not split. ✘
  • Choice C — A convergence: a coming together of separate things at one point — directly describes two broods with different cycles meeting simultaneously. ✔
  • Choice D — An expiration: an ending or dying out — this implies the cicadas stopped or died, which contradicts the passage describing their emergence and spread. ✘
That matches choice C — C is correct.
